titleStmt.title: Letter from Patrick Carey to Messres Helys Ltd, 29 November 1915
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Patrick Carey

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
Patrick Carey (1853-1934) was the Union Clerk for the Killarney Poor Law Board of Guardians. Poor law unions and their workhouses were established by the Poor Law (Ireland) Act, 1838, as a response to widespread poverty, and were each governed by a Board of Guardians. This letter comes from the Killarney Poor Law Board of Guardians Minute Book.
Messrs Helys was a printworks operating from Dame Lane from 1897 up to the 1960s. Under the control of Charles Wisdom Hely (1856-1929) Hely's printworks were imortalised in James Joyce's Ulysses. In 1916 Helys was performing a balancing act between entertaining Irish nationalists and the British military authorities. After printing several recruitment posters for the British the company were warned to cease printing "British propaganda". Shortly after the printers would produce a commemorative booklet “The ‘Sinn Fein’ Revolt, Illustrated” (1916) along with a series of postcards documenting the destruction of the GPO during Easter Week.

msIdentifier.collection: Killarney Poor Law Board of Guardians BG104/B/4

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
29th Novr. 1915 Gentelmen, I am directed by the Board of Guardians
of above Union to inform you that they have accepted
your tender (at the price specified therein) for the
supply of Stationery to the 30th September 1916. Owing to the proposed introduction of
the new Dietary Scale and the consequent alteration in
the forms at present in use, the Guardians have decided
not to not to enter into a Contract for the supply of
Bound Books for the current year, Yours faithfully
Clerk of Union. Messrs Helys Ltd,
Dame St, Dublin,
